does: This paper presents a case study of Intercom's approach to building and scaling an AI system for customer support. The authors share three foundational lessons learned from their engineering practice: establishing robust evaluation frameworks to measure AI system performance, designing modular architecture that allows rapid iteration and model updates, and implementing feedback mechanisms that integrate user signals into continuous improvement cycles. The work focuses on practical implementation details rather than novel algorithms, demonstrating how organizational structure and engineering discipline enabled Intercom to maintain competitive advantage as AI capabilities evolved rapidly.
connects: This work builds on longstanding research in machine learning systems engineering and production ML, extending earlier work on MLOps practices, model evaluation methodologies, and feedback loops in deployed systems. It responds to the gap between academic AI research and real-world deployment challenges that have been highlighted in recent ML systems literature. The emphasis on evaluation frameworks connects to prior work on benchmarking and robustness testing, while the modular architecture approach relates to established software engineering best practices adapted for machine learning contexts, particularly work on maintaining and evolving ML systems in production environments.
enables: The paper points toward future work in sustainable AI development where organizations can maintain technological advantages through systematic processes rather than isolated breakthroughs. It enables better practices for customer support AI by demonstrating that architectural flexibility and continuous evaluation create pathways for adopting emerging models without full system redesign. The lessons potentially generalize to other domain-specific AI applications, suggesting frameworks for other companies building long-term AI capabilities. This positions customer support as a domain where iterative, evaluation-driven AI development can compound advantages over time.
